project.name: LLM Gateway
project.description: LLM API security proxy gateway — intercepts, scans, and audits all OpenAI-compatible API requests

sprints.name: Phase 7 - Admin Dashboard
sprints.goal: Build a complete admin dashboard with React + shadcn/ui dark theme, serving as the management UI for LLM Gateway. Includes backend API expansion (config, providers, health, security endpoints), frontend scaffold (Vite + React 18 + Tailwind + shadcn/ui), 7 pages (Login, Overview, Keys, Audit, Security, Providers, Settings), authentication flow, and production deployment via @fastify/static.

features.title: Add healthTracker server decoration and type declaration
features.description: Mount KeyHealthTracker instance as a Fastify server decoration (server.healthTracker) so admin routes can read provider key health data. Add type declaration in src/types.ts. Instantiate in src/index.ts after the health tracker is created in the proxy layer.
acceptance_criteria: Given src/types.ts is updated, when server.healthTracker is accessed in a route handler, then TypeScript compiles without error
Given the server starts, when server.healthTracker is inspected, then it is a KeyHealthTracker instance
Given pnpm typecheck runs, then no type errors
features.technical_notes: Follow existing pattern for server.rateLimiter and server.config decorations. The KeyHealthTracker is already instantiated in the proxy layer — extract and mount as decoration. Add 'healthTracker' to FastifyInstance via module augmentation in src/types.ts.

features.title: Create admin config and providers API endpoints
features.description: Create src/routes/admin/config.ts with three endpoints: GET /admin/config (returns sanitized runtime config — no encryption_key, admin_token, or provider API keys), GET /admin/providers (lists all providers with keyCount instead of actual keys), GET /admin/providers/health (returns key health from server.healthTracker, grouped by provider, using key-N id format). All endpoints reuse the existing admin token auth hook pattern.
acceptance_criteria: Given GET /admin/config is called with valid admin token, then response contains port, host, log_level, default_rpm/tpm/rpd, security, retry fields
Given GET /admin/config response, when inspected, then encryption_key, admin_token, and provider apiKey/apiKeys are NOT present
Given GET /admin/providers is called, then response contains providers array with name, baseUrl, keyStrategy, keyCount, modelMappings, isDefault
Given GET /admin/providers/health is called, then response contains providers with keys array showing id (key-N), avgLatency, consecutiveErrors, isHealthy
Given any admin endpoint is called without auth, then 401 is returned
Given pnpm typecheck && pnpm test runs, then all existing tests pass
features.technical_notes: Create new file src/routes/admin/config.ts. Use FastifyPluginCallback pattern matching existing admin routes. Add admin token auth hook (same as keys.ts/audit.ts). For /admin/config: read from server.config, construct sanitized response. For /admin/providers: read server.config.providers, map to safe shape with keyCount. For /admin/providers/health: need to iterate providers and call server.healthTracker.getHealth() for each key. The health tracker currently uses the raw API key string as the map key — need to add a method that returns all tracked data or iterate by provider config. Register plugin in src/index.ts. Add OpenAPI schema definitions in src/schemas/admin/config.ts.

features.title: Add security stats aggregation to AuditStore and endpoint
features.description: Add querySecurityStats(startDate?, endDate?) method to src/db/audit-store.ts that computes: blockedRequests count, piiDetections with byType breakdown (parsing pii_types_found JSON field), injectionAttempts with avgScore and scoreDistribution (0.2 intervals), contentFilter with allowed/flagged/blocked counts. Add GET /admin/audit/security endpoint in src/routes/admin/audit.ts that exposes this data.
acceptance_criteria: Given GET /admin/audit/security is called, then response contains blockedRequests, piiDetections {total, byType}, injectionAttempts {total, avgScore, scoreDistribution}, contentFilter {allowed, flagged, blocked}
Given GET /admin/audit/security?startDate=2026-04-01&endDate=2026-04-13, then only logs within the date range are aggregated
Given audit_logs with pii_types_found='["EMAIL","PHONE"]', when byType is computed, then EMAIL and PHONE are each incremented
Given audit_logs with prompt_injection_score values, when scoreDistribution is computed, then values are bucketed into 5 intervals (0-0.2, 0.2-0.4, 0.4-0.6, 0.6-0.8, 0.8-1.0)
Given pnpm typecheck && pnpm test runs, then all tests pass
features.technical_notes: The pii_types_found field stores JSON string like '["EMAIL","PHONE"]' — parse with JSON.parse() and aggregate. For scoreDistribution, use CASE WHEN SQL to bucket prompt_injection_score values. Content filter logic: allowed = success AND no PII AND no injection score; flagged = success AND (PII OR injection); blocked = status='blocked'. Add unit tests for the new AuditStore method. Add OpenAPI schema in src/schemas/admin/audit.ts.

features.title: Scaffold Vite + React 18 + Tailwind + shadcn/ui admin project
features.description: Create admin/ directory as an independent Vite project with: package.json (react 18, react-dom, react-router-dom v6, tailwindcss v3, @tanstack/react-query v5, recharts, lucide-react, date-fns, shadcn/ui deps), tsconfig.json (strict, JSX, paths), vite.config.ts (dev proxy to localhost:3000), tailwind.config.ts (dark theme config matching design system), postcss.config.js, index.html, src/main.tsx entry point. Initialize shadcn/ui with dark theme.
acceptance_criteria: Given admin/ directory, when pnpm install && pnpm dev runs, then Vite dev server starts on port 5173 without errors
Given the Vite config, when /admin/* requests are made during dev, then they are proxied to http://localhost:3000
Given tailwind.config.ts, when dark theme CSS variables are used, then they render correctly
Given shadcn/ui is initialized, when a Button component is imported, then it renders with dark theme styling
Given admin/tsconfig.json, when pnpm build runs, then TypeScript compiles without errors
features.technical_notes: Use pnpm create vite with React + TypeScript template, then customize. Tailwind v3 with shadcn/ui init: npx shadcn-ui@latest init (select: dark theme, CSS variables, default style). Key deps from design spec: react@^18.3, react-dom@^18.3, react-router-dom@^6.28, @tanstack/react-query@^5.62, @tanstack/react-table@^8.20, recharts@^2.15, lucide-react@^0.460, date-fns@^3.6, class-variance-authority@^0.7, clsx@^2.1, tailwind-merge@^2.6. DevDeps: @vitejs/plugin-react@^4.3, tailwindcss@^3.4, postcss@^8.4, autoprefixer@^10.4, typescript@^5.6, vite@^5.4. Configure path alias @/ -> src/ in both tsconfig and vite config.

features.title: Implement API client, auth module, and core utilities
features.description: Create admin/src/lib/api-client.ts (fetch wrapper with auto Bearer token, 401 auto-redirect, baseURL from VITE_API_BASE), admin/src/lib/auth.ts (token get/set/clear with localStorage key 'llm-gw-admin-token'), admin/src/lib/utils.ts (number abbreviation, USD formatting, ms formatting, date formatting helpers using date-fns), and admin/src/hooks/use-auth.ts (React context for auth state).
acceptance_criteria: Given api-client is used, when a request is made, then Authorization: Bearer <token> header is automatically added
Given api-client receives a 401 response, when the response is processed, then token is cleared and user is redirected to /login
Given auth.ts stores a token, when getToken() is called, then it returns the stored value
Given utils formatNumber(1500000), when called, then it returns '1.5M'
Given utils formatUsd(0.0423), when called, then it returns '$0.0423'
Given use-auth hook is used in a component, when logout() is called, then token is cleared and user navigates to /login
features.technical_notes: api-client.ts: Export a singleton object with get/post/patch/del methods. Each method wraps fetch with: baseURL prefix, JSON headers, Authorization header from auth.ts, response intercept for 401. baseURL defaults to '' (same-origin). auth.ts: Simple get/set/clear for localStorage. use-auth.ts: Create AuthContext with token, isAuthenticated, login(token), logout(). Provider wraps App. Login calls GET /admin/config to verify token before storing.

features.title: Build layout shell: Sidebar, Navbar, AuthGuard, routing
features.description: Create the app layout skeleton with: admin/src/App.tsx (react-router v6 routes), admin/src/components/layout/sidebar.tsx (collapsible sidebar with navigation items matching design — Home, Keys, Audit, Security, Providers, Settings), admin/src/components/layout/navbar.tsx (floating top bar with logo, health indicator, logout button), admin/src/components/layout/page-header.tsx (page title + breadcrumb), admin/src/components/layout/auth-guard.tsx (redirects to /login if no token). Must use ui-ux-pro-max skill for all component implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Given the app loads without a token, when any protected route is accessed, then user is redirected to /login
Given the sidebar, when toggle button is clicked, then it collapses to 64px icon-only mode
Given the sidebar, when a nav item is clicked, then the corresponding route is navigated and the item shows active state highlight
Given the navbar, when rendered, then it shows 'LLM Gateway' logo on the left and logout button on the right
Given a protected page renders, when page-header is shown, then it displays the page title and breadcrumb
Given the app on mobile (<768px), when sidebar toggle is clicked, then sidebar appears as an overlay
features.technical_notes: IMPORTANT: Use /ui-ux-pro-max skill for implementation. Design spec: dark theme (zinc-950 bg, zinc-800 borders, indigo-500 accent). Sidebar: fixed left, 240px expanded / 64px collapsed, collapsible via button. Items: Home (LayoutDashboard icon), Keys (Key icon), Audit (ScrollText icon), Security (Shield icon), Providers (Server icon), Settings (Settings icon). Navbar: fixed top, h-16, floating style, right side has logout button. AuthGuard: wrapper component that checks auth context, redirects to /login if no token. Use shadcn/ui Sheet for mobile sidebar overlay. React Router v6 with Outlet for nested layout.

features.title: Build Login page
features.description: Create admin/src/pages/login.tsx — a centered card layout with a single admin token input field. On submit, calls GET /admin/config to validate the token. On success, stores token and redirects to /. On 401, shows 'Token invalid' error. If already authenticated, redirects to /. Must use ui-ux-pro-max skill for implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Given the login page, when rendered, then a centered card with 'LLM Gateway Admin' title and token input is shown
Given a valid token is entered, when submitted, then GET /admin/config is called and on 200 user is redirected to /
Given an invalid token, when submitted, then 'Token invalid' error message is displayed
Given the user is already authenticated, when visiting /login, then they are redirected to /
Given the submit button, when request is in flight, then loading state is shown and button is disabled
features.technical_notes: IMPORTANT: Use /ui-ux-pro-max skill for implementation. Design: dark bg, centered card (max-w-sm), Input with password toggle (Eye/EyeOff icons), Submit button with loading spinner. Use shadcn/ui Card, Input, Button, Label components. Call apiClient.get('/admin/config') for validation. Store token via auth context login() on success. Use react-router useNavigate for redirect.

features.title: Build Overview Dashboard page
features.description: Create admin/src/pages/overview.tsx with: 4 KPI cards (Total Requests, Token Usage, Total Cost, Avg Latency) with delta change indicators, By Model bar chart, By Status pie chart, Recent Activity table (5 rows), PII Detection Rate donut progress. Data from GET /admin/audit/stats + GET /admin/audit/logs?limit=5. Must use ui-ux-pro-max skill for all component and chart implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Given the overview page loads, when data is fetched, then 4 KPI cards display correct values with delta indicators
Given KPI delta change, when compared with previous period, then up/down arrows with green/red colors are shown
Given audit stats contain byModel data, when the bar chart renders, then each model appears as a labeled bar
Given audit stats contain byStatus data, when the pie chart renders, then success/error/blocked segments with correct colors (green/red/orange) are shown
Given recent activity data, when the table renders, then 5 most recent rows show time, model, tokens, cost, status
Given piiDetectionRate from stats, when donut renders, then percentage is displayed with color-coded progress
features.technical_notes: IMPORTANT: Use /ui-ux-pro-max skill for implementation. Create TanStack Query hooks in admin/src/hooks/use-audit-logs.ts and use-audit-stats.ts (or reuse a single use-config.ts). KPI Cards: shadcn/ui Card with Lucide icon, formatted number (utils.formatNumber), delta badge (green arrow-up / red arrow-down). Charts: recharts BarChart, PieChart, custom Donut. Use design system colors: indigo-500 accent, green-500 success, red-500 error, orange-500 blocked. Delta: call stats twice (current period vs previous period) and compute diff. Responsive: 4 cards -> 2x2 grid on tablet, stacked on mobile.

features.title: Build API Key Management page
features.description: Create admin/src/pages/keys.tsx with: paginated DataTable of keys (Name, ID, Key Prefix, Rate Limits, Created, Status), Create Key dialog with form, Edit Key side Sheet, Revoke confirmation AlertDialog, post-creation key display dialog (shows key once with copy button). Must use ui-ux-pro-max skill for all component implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Given the keys page loads, when data is fetched from GET /admin/keys, then a paginated table displays all keys
Given the table, when sorted by a column header, then data re-sorts accordingly
Given 'Create Key' button is clicked, when dialog opens, then name (required) and optional rate limits fields are shown
Given a key is created, when response returns, then a dialog shows the raw key value with a copy button and a warning that it won't be shown again
Given a key row, when edit is clicked, then a Sheet slides in from the right with editable name and rate limits
Given a key row, when revoke is clicked, then AlertDialog asks for confirmation before DELETE request
Given a key with revoked_at !== null, when displayed, then Status Badge shows 'Revoked' in red
features.technical_notes: IMPORTANT: Use /ui-ux-pro-max skill for implementation. Use TanStack Table v8 wrapped in shadcn/ui Table. Create admin/src/hooks/use-keys.ts with useQuery for list, useMutation for create/edit/revoke. DataTable component: admin/src/components/data-table.tsx — reusable with column definitions. Status Badge: Active (green), Revoked (red). Create dialog: shadcn/ui Dialog + Form inputs. Edit: shadcn/ui Sheet (side panel). Revoke: shadcn/ui AlertDialog. Post-creation: Dialog with monospace key text + Copy button (navigator.clipboard). Pagination: server-side via offset/limit params. Table columns: Name (string), ID (truncated with copy), Key Prefix (monospace), Rate Limits (RPM/TPM/RPD badges), Created (relative date), Status (badge).

features.title: Build Audit Log Browser page
features.description: Create admin/src/pages/audit.tsx with: filter bar (date range, model select, endpoint select, status select, key select), paginated DataTable of logs, Status Badge (success/error/blocked), PII Indicator badge with tooltip, Injection Score progress bar with color gradient, detail Sheet drawer on row click, CSV export button. Must use ui-ux-pro-max skill for all component implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Given the audit page loads, when data is fetched, then a paginated log table renders with all columns
Given filter bar inputs, when date range is set, then only matching logs are shown
Given filter bar, when model/status/endpoint/key dropdown is selected, then table filters accordingly
Given a log row with pii_detected=true, when rendered, then PII badge shows with tooltip listing detected types
Given a log row with prompt_injection_score=0.8, when rendered, then a red progress bar shows 80%
Given a log row is clicked, when detail Sheet opens, then full log data is displayed
Given CSV export button is clicked, when data is exported, then a CSV file downloads with all visible columns
features.technical_notes: IMPORTANT: Use /ui-ux-pro-max skill for implementation. Filter bar: shadcn/ui Select + Popover + Calendar for date range. Model/key options from /admin/audit/stats.byModel and /admin/keys. Table columns: Time (formatted with date-fns), Request ID (truncated, monospace), Key (key_prefix), Model, Tokens (prompt+completion), Cost (USD formatted), Latency (ms), Status (badge: green/red/orange), PII (badge+tooltip). Injection Score: custom progress bar component with green->orange->red gradient based on score. Detail Sheet: shadcn/ui Sheet with all log fields including content_hash_sha256. CSV export: admin/src/pages/audit/export.ts — fetch all pages, generate CSV client-side, trigger download via Blob URL.

features.title: Build Security Monitor page
features.description: Create admin/src/pages/security.tsx with: 4 Security KPI cards (Blocked Requests, PII Detections, Injection Attempts, Content Filter breakdown), Threat Feed table (filtered audit logs showing blocked/security events), PII Type horizontal bar chart, Injection Score distribution histogram. Data from GET /admin/audit/security + GET /admin/audit/logs?status=blocked. Must use ui-ux-pro-max skill for all component and chart implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Given the security page loads, when data is fetched, then 4 KPI cards display blockedRequests, piiDetections.total, injectionAttempts.total, and contentFilter totals
Given KPI cards have trend data, when compared with previous period, then delta indicators are shown
Given the threat feed table, when blocked events are fetched, then they display in a table with time, model, status, PII info
Given piiDetections.byType data, when horizontal bar chart renders, then each PII type appears as a labeled bar
Given injectionAttempts.scoreDistribution data, when histogram renders, then 5 score buckets are shown as bars
features.technical_notes: IMPORTANT: Use /ui-ux-pro-max skill for implementation. Create admin/src/hooks/use-audit-security.ts with TanStack Query. KPI cards: same pattern as Overview but security-specific. Threat Feed: reuse DataTable component with status=blocked filter. PII Type chart: recharts horizontal BarChart with labels. Score Distribution: custom histogram with 5 bins. Content Filter KPI: show three numbers (allowed/flagged/blocked) in a single card with colored indicators. Trend: two calls to /admin/audit/security with different date ranges.

features.title: Build Provider/Model Status page
features.description: Create admin/src/pages/providers.tsx with: Provider Cards grid (one card per provider showing name, baseUrl, health indicator, key strategy badge, key count, avg latency), Model Mappings table (alias -> real model), Key Usage distribution bars, and Topology View (SVG flow diagram: Provider -> Model Mappings -> Key health nodes). Data from GET /admin/providers + GET /admin/providers/health. Must use ui-ux-pro-max skill for all component implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Given the providers page loads, when data is fetched, then a card grid shows one card per configured provider
Given a provider card, when health data is loaded, then a green/yellow/red indicator reflects aggregated key health
Given a provider card, when modelMappings exist, then a table shows alias -> real model name mappings
Given a provider card, when key health data is loaded, then usage bars show relative latency across keys
Given the topology view, when rendered, then an SVG diagram shows Provider -> Model -> Key health status flow
features.technical_notes: IMPORTANT: Use /ui-ux-pro-max skill for implementation. Create admin/src/hooks/use-providers.ts with TanStack Query for both /admin/providers and /admin/providers/health. Provider Card: shadcn/ui Card with health indicator dot (green=all healthy, yellow=some unhealthy, red=all unhealthy), keyStrategy badge, keyCount, avg latency. Model Mappings: simple table within card. Key Usage: horizontal bars showing avgLatency per key, colored by health. Topology: admin/src/pages/providers/topology.tsx — SVG-based flow diagram with nodes for providers, model mappings, and keys, edges showing relationships, node colors based on health status.

features.title: Build Settings/Config page
features.description: Create admin/src/pages/settings.tsx with read-only display of current configuration: General config (port, host, log_level, default rate limits), Security Rules (injection_threshold, blocked/flagged PII types), Retry Policy (max_retries, delays, backoff), Provider Config details, and TOML Preview (generated from JSON data). Data from GET /admin/config + GET /admin/providers. Must use ui-ux-pro-max skill for all component implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Given the settings page loads, when config data is fetched, then General, Security, Retry, Provider sections are displayed
Given each config section, when rendered, then key-value pairs are shown in a description list format
Given security rules, when displayed, then blocked_pii_types and flagged_pii_types are shown as colored badge lists
Given the TOML preview, when rendered, then a code block shows the config formatted as TOML text with monospace font and syntax highlighting
features.technical_notes: IMPORTANT: Use /ui-ux-pro-max skill for implementation. Create admin/src/hooks/use-config.ts with TanStack Query for GET /admin/config. Each section: shadcn/ui Card with DescriptionList (label-value pairs). Security rules: badges for PII types (red for blocked, amber for flagged). TOML Preview: CodeBlock component with JetBrains Mono font, dark background, formatted TOML generated from JSON data (write a simple JSON-to-TOML formatter or just display key-value pairs in TOML-like format). Provider section: reuse provider cards or show as nested config.

features.title: Production deployment: @fastify/static integration and build pipeline
features.description: Create src/plugins/serve-admin.ts that registers @fastify/static to serve admin/dist/ at /admin prefix with SPA fallback (non-API requests return index.html). Add @fastify/static to main package.json dependencies. Configure admin/ build script. Ensure the plugin only activates when admin/dist/ exists. Add build verification.
acceptance_criteria: Given admin/dist/ exists, when GET /admin is requested, then index.html is served
Given admin/dist/ exists, when GET /admin/assets/main-abc123.js is requested, then the JS file is served with 1-year cache
Given admin/dist/ exists, when GET /admin/keys is requested (SPA route), then index.html is served
Given admin/dist/ does NOT exist, when the server starts, then admin static serving is skipped with no error
Given admin/ has pnpm build run, when admin/dist/ is inspected, then index.html and JS/CSS bundles exist
Given the server serves admin static files, when GET /admin/config is requested, then the API endpoint responds (not static file)
features.technical_notes: Install @fastify/static in main project. Create src/plugins/serve-admin.ts: check if admin/dist/ directory exists (fs.existsSync), if yes register @fastify/static with root: resolve('admin/dist'), prefix: '/admin', serve: true, decorateReply: false. Add a wildcard GET /admin/* route AFTER static registration that serves index.html for SPA fallback — but BEFORE the admin API routes (or use a constraint to only serve static for non-API paths). The admin API routes (/admin/keys, /admin/audit/*, /admin/config, /admin/providers) must take precedence over static serving. Strategy: register static plugin AFTER all admin API routes, with a wildcard route that checks if the request is for a static file, and falls back to index.html. Register in src/index.ts.

features.title: Integration tests and end-to-end verification
features.description: Write backend tests for the 4 new admin endpoints (config, providers, providers/health, audit/security). Verify the admin frontend builds successfully. Test the @fastify/static integration. Run full test suite to confirm no regressions.
acceptance_criteria: Given GET /admin/config is called in tests, then sanitized config is returned without secrets
Given GET /admin/providers is called in tests, then provider list with keyCount is returned
Given GET /admin/providers/health is called in tests, then health data is returned
Given GET /admin/audit/security is called in tests, then aggregated security stats are returned with correct structure
Given pnpm typecheck && pnpm test runs in main project, then all tests pass
Given admin/ pnpm build runs, then dist/ is produced without errors
features.technical_notes: Create tests/integration/admin-config.test.ts (or add to existing admin test files). Use createTestServer() helper. Test each new endpoint: happy path with valid token, 401 without token, response structure validation. For /admin/audit/security: seed test data in audit_logs and verify aggregation. For /admin/providers/health: the healthTracker may have no data in tests — verify it returns empty arrays. Add backend unit tests for AuditStore.querySecurityStats(). Verify admin/ builds: cd admin && pnpm build.
